title: UML / Architecture
description: Technical reference for bootstrap, lifecycle, configuration, middleware and layer boundaries in the live framework.

shell.title: Architecture Reference
shell.description: Browse the canonical runtime model without leaving the current shell.

rail.scope_description: Primary areas documented in this diagnostic surface.
rail.notes_description: Intent and reading rules for this page.

notes_items.runtime_truth: These diagrams describe the real runtime, not an aspirational architecture.
notes_items.web_stack: Focus stays on the traditional PHP web stack used by Catalyst.
notes_items.mermaid: Mermaid is used as a readable reference, backed by the current implementation.

bootstrap.title: Bootstrap Chain - Server to Kernel
bootstrap.description: Every web request passes through this chain before application code runs. The server auto-prepends error-catcher.php, which loads bootstrap constants, resolves .env, mirrors ConfigManager into global state and installs PHP error handlers before index.php executes.
bootstrap.diagram:
flowchart TD
    subgraph SRV ["Server Layer"]
        APACHE(["Apache / PHP-FPM"])
        HT[".htaccess
php_value auto_prepend_file
-> boot-core/requirement-loader/error-catcher.php

Also: HTTPS redirect · mod_rewrite
Security headers · index.php fallback"]
        UI[".user.ini
auto_prepend_file - non-Apache fallback
(commented by default - enable manually)"]
        APACHE --> HT
        HT -. "non-Apache hosts" .-> UI
    end

    HT --> EC

    subgraph BOOT ["Boot Chain - error-catcher.php"]
        EC["error-catcher.php
Guard: INITIALIZED_BUG_CATCHER
Prevents double initialization"]
        EC --> SC["sys-constant.php
PHP 8.4 version check
DS · PD · IS_CLI · IS_REQUEST
TW · NL · LOG_DIR
Guard: LOADED_SYS_CONSTANT"]
        SC --> SPL["spl-autoload.php
PSR-4 class map registration
(pre-Composer bootstrap only)"]
        SPL --> EVC["env-constant.php
Reads: boot-core/config/env/.env
Throws EnvironmentException if missing
Copies .env.example -> .env if found
ENV · IS_DEVELOPMENT/STAGING/TESTING/PRODUCTION
GET_ENV_VAR · DB_* · ROUTE_CACHE
IS_CONFIGURED · DISPLAY_LOGS
Guard: LOADED_ENV_CONSTANT"]
        EVC --> CFG["ConfigManager bootstrap
ConfigManager::getInstance()
$GLOBALS['APP_CONFIGURATION']"]
        CFG --> ECL["ErrorCatcher.php
set_error_handler()
set_exception_handler()
register_shutdown_function()
Captures fatal errors · dev dump · prod log"]
    end

    ECL --> IDX

    subgraph ENTRY ["Entry Point - public/index.php"]
        IDX["index.php
Checks IS_CLI -> exit(1) if CLI
require_once vendor/autoload.php
-> Composer PSR-4 autoloader
-> dump-function.php (global helpers: e(), ms(), mi()...)"]
        IDX --> KRN["Kernel::getInstance()
  ->bootstrap()
  ->run()"]
    end

    KRN --> KBS

    subgraph KBS ["Kernel::bootstrap()"]
        B1["applyRuntimeConfiguration()
app.json -> debug/timezone
logging.json -> Logger config"]
        B1 --> B2["SessionManager::init()
session.json > .env fallback
Strict mode · configurable domain
gc_maxlifetime alignment"]
        B2 --> B3["Translator::init(app.project.project_lang, boot-core/lang/)
Lazy JSON load · in-memory cache"]
        B3 --> B4["loadRoutes()
GlobalMiddlewareRegistrar + ModuleViewPathRegistrar
Always before route cache lookup
Cache policy -> cache.json only
Runtime route cache allowed only in production"]
        B4 --> RC{"cache_enabled &&
route_cache &&
IS_PRODUCTION?"}
        RC -- "Yes" --> CR["loadCachedRoutes()
PD/boot-core/cache/routes.cache.php
Route::__set_state() + RouteCollection::__set_state()"]
        RC -- "No / miss" --> MR["CliRouteLoader::routeFiles()
boot-core/routes/global-routes.php
boot-core/routes/api.php
Repository/Framework/*/routes.php
Repository/App/Surface/*/routes.php"]
        MR --> RB["Best effort route cache rebuild
when route_cache is enabled"]
    end

lifecycle.title: Request Lifecycle - Kernel::run to Response
lifecycle.description: After bootstrap completes, Kernel::run() wraps dispatch in SecurityHeadersMiddleware and hands off to the global middleware pipeline. Global middleware now runs before route matching, so early handlers such as CORS preflight can terminate the request before a 405 check.
lifecycle.diagram:
flowchart TD
    KRN(["Kernel::run()"])

    subgraph GMID ["Global Middleware Pipeline"]
        GM0["SecurityHeadersMiddleware\nKernel level wrapper · CSP nonce · HSTS · X-Frame-Options\nX-Content-Type-Options · Referrer-Policy"]
        GMCP["CanonicalPathRedirectMiddleware\nNormalizes request paths"]
        GMTN["TenancyContextMiddleware\nResolves tenant context"]
        GMRT["RequestThrottlingMiddleware\nMutating requests\nProfile per route or default"]
        GM1["CorsMiddleware\neffective CORS config · FeatureFlagInterface\nOPTIONS preflight -> 204\nOrigin allowlist + fnmatch()"]
        GM2["WebSocketBootMiddleware\neffective websocket config · feature flag\nAuto boots WS process when enabled"]
        GM3["SetupMiddleware\nIS_CONFIGURED check\nRedirects to /configuration/environment-setup on first boot"]
        GM4["CsrfMiddleware\nPOST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH only\nToken validation via CsrfProtection"]
        GM0 --> GM1 --> GMCP --> GM2 --> GMTN --> GM3 --> GMRT --> GM4
    end

    KRN --> GM0
    GM4 --> RD

    subgraph DISPATCH ["Router::dispatch()"]
        RD["Router resolves URI + Method\nagainst RouteCollection"]
        RD --> RM{Route match?}
        RM -- "No match" --> E404["RouteNotFoundException\nbuildNotFoundResponse 404\nError template or JSON"]
        RM -- "Method mismatch" --> E405["MethodNotAllowedException\nbuildMethodNotAllowedResponse 405"]
        RM -- "Matched" --> RMID["Route middleware chain\n(per route, in registration order)"]
    end

    subgraph RMIDS ["Route Middleware (optional, per route)"]
        R1["AuthMiddleware\nsession check -> redirect /login"]
        R2["GuestMiddleware\nauthenticated -> redirect /"]
        R3["RoleMiddleware\nGate::allows() -> 403 if denied"]
        R4["SetupGuardMiddleware\nfirst run: setup access
after configuration: authenticated privileged access"]
        R5["LoginThrottleMiddleware\n5 attempts / 10 min / IP\nfile based SHA-256 tracking"]
    end

    RMID --> R1
    RMID --> R2
    RMID --> R3
    RMID --> R4
    RMID --> R5

    R1 & R2 & R3 & R4 & R5 --> CTRL

    subgraph CTRLS ["Controller Layer"]
        CTRL["Controller::method(Request)\nOrchestrates validate · DB · Session\nFlash · Notify · Redirect"]
        CTRL --> RT{Response type}
        RT -- "HTML" --> VW["View::render(template, data)\nboot-core/template/document.phtml + shell.phtml\nfindTemplate() -> registered view paths\nPartials, components, e() escaping"]
        RT -- "JSON" --> JS["JsonResponse\n{success, message, data/errors}\nwithNotification() · withRedirect()"]
        RT -- "Redirect" --> RR["302 Response\nwithSuccess() / withError() flash"]
        RT -- "HtmlResponse" --> HR["HtmlResponse\nRaw HTML string"]
    end

    VW --> RS
    JS --> RS
    RR --> RS
    HR --> RS
    E404 --> RS
    E405 --> RS
    RS(["Response::send()\nHTTP status · headers · body"])

constants.title: Bootstrap Constants Reference
constants.description: All PHP constants are defined before index.php runs through error-catcher.php -> sys-constant.php -> env-constant.php. They are available globally across the framework without import.

rows.ds: DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR - platform safe path delimiter (/ on Linux, \\ on Windows).
rows.pd: Project root absolute path resolved from boot-core/constant/ up to the repository root.
rows.is_cli: true in CLI execution; guards command-only code paths.
rows.is_request: Inverse of IS_CLI; used by request-aware helpers such as debug output.
rows.tw: Terminal width in CLI only; defaults to 80 when detection fails.
rows.nl:
PHP_EOL in CLI,
in web output.
rows.log_dir: Absolute path to boot-core/storage/logs/ where Logger writes files.
rows.loaded_sys_constant: Guard flag that prevents double loading of sys-constant.php.

env.description: Reads boot-core/config/env/.env. Throws EnvironmentException if the file is missing or empty. If available, .env.example is copied first. Values are also exposed in GET_ENV_VAR[key].

rows.env: APP_ENV raw environment name: development, staging, testing or production.
rows.get_env_var: Full associative array of parsed .env key=value pairs. CSV values become sub-arrays.
rows.is_configured: Derived from app.json -> project_config; true after Setup completes.
rows.display_logs: APP_DISPLAY_LOGS fallback from .env until effective logging config is applied.
rows.route_cache: Deprecated compatibility constant. Runtime activation now lives only in cache.json.
rows.db_database: DB_DATABASE from .env; database or schema name.
rows.db_password: DB_PASSWORD from .env; database password and never logged.
rows.loaded_env_constant: Guard flag that prevents double loading of env-constant.php.

config.title: Config Flow - JSON Priority Chain
config.description: ConfigManager::getInstance() loads once per request. Defaults are compiled from .env for bootstrap compatibility, while runtime cache activation belongs to cache.json. Per-environment JSON files override defaults section by section and the effective configuration is mirrored into global runtime state.
config.diagram:
flowchart TD
    ENV_FILE["boot-core/config/env/.env\nRaw key=value pairs\nenv-constant.php reads once at boot"]
    ENV_FILE --> CONST["PHP Constants\nENV, IS_DEVELOPMENT, DB_HOST, ...\nAvailable globally without import"]
    CONST --> CM
    CM --> GLOB["$GLOBALS['APP_CONFIGURATION']\nEarly bootstrap mirror"]

    subgraph CM ["ConfigManager::getInstance() - singleton"]
        DEF["readDefaults()\nCompiles baseline from GET_ENV_VAR\nSections: app, db, mail, ftp,\nsession, logging,\nsecurity, websocket, cors, devtools\nCache defaults stay setup owned and disabled"]
        DEF --> LOAD["load()\nOptional compiled config cache first\nThen glob boot-core/config/{env}/*.json\nJSON decoded -> section array\nThrows on malformed JSON"]
        LOAD --> MERGE["entry()\nJSON named entry overrides\n.env derived defaults"]
    end

    MERGE --> READY["config[ section ][ key ]\nFinal resolved configuration"]

    subgraph CONSUMERS ["Consumers (read only)"]
        C1["MailManager\nconfig['mail']"]
        C2["SessionManager\nconfig['session']"]
        C3["CorsMiddleware\nconfig['cors']"]
        C4["WebSocketBootMiddleware\nconfig['websocket']"]
        C5["Kernel / Router / CacheManager\nconfig['cache']\nproduction only runtime activation"]
        C6["Controllers / Services\nconfig['app'], config['security']..."]
    end

    READY --> C1 & C2 & C3 & C4 & C5 & C6

rows.app: Kernel, SecurityHeadersMiddleware, OAuth/Auth email links, WebSocketToken.
rows.ftp: Setup FTP/SFTP credentials and upload pretest surface; downstream deployment consumer still pending.
rows.cache: CacheSettings, Kernel, Router, CacheManager and bootstrap cache artifacts; setup-owned activation only.
rows.security: AuthManager such as bcrypt rounds and MFA flag.
rows.websocket: WebSocketBootMiddleware, WebSocketPublisher and websocket server bootstrap.
rows.devtools: Legacy compatibility mirror only; runtime now derives from app/logging while cache activation lives in cache.json.

config.priority_note: Priority: JSON file value > .env default > hard-coded fallback. JSON files exist only after Setup completes. On first boot, only .env defaults are available and cache stays off until setup persists cache.json.

middleware.title: Middleware Stack - Execution Order
middleware.description: Middleware runs in two layers: global for every request and route level for selected routes. Both layers use the MiddlewareInterface::process(Request, Closure): Response contract.
middleware.diagram:
flowchart TD
    REQ(["Incoming Request"])
    REQ --> SH

    subgraph GLOBAL ["Global Middleware - every request"]
        SH["SecurityHeadersMiddleware
CSP nonce generation
HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy
X-Content-Type-Options"]
        SH --> CO["CorsMiddleware
Origin check via effective CORS config
OPTIONS preflight -> 204 early return
Credentials + max_age headers"]
        CO --> CP["CanonicalPathRedirectMiddleware
Normalizes request paths"]
        CP --> WS["WebSocketBootMiddleware
Feature gated by effective websocket config
Auto boots only when enabled"]
        WS --> TC["TenancyContextMiddleware
Resolves tenant context"]
        TC --> SM["SetupMiddleware
Checks IS_CONFIGURED
Redirects to /configuration/environment-setup if not configured
Skipped when already on /configuration/environment-setup"]
        SM --> RTG["RequestThrottlingMiddleware
Mutating requests
Profile per route or default"]
        RTG --> CS["CsrfMiddleware
POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH only
Token from header or POST body
Rejects mismatches -> 403 JSON / redirect"]
    end

    CS --> RL

    subgraph RL ["Route Level Middleware (per route, optional)"]
        AM["AuthMiddleware
Session check: auth user present?
No -> redirect /login with ?redirect=
Yes -> continues"]
        GM["GuestMiddleware
Inverse of Auth: authenticated?
Yes -> redirect to /
No -> continues"]
        RM["RoleMiddleware
Gate::allows(role) check
Denied -> 403 Forbidden response"]
        LT["LoginThrottleMiddleware
5 attempts / 10 min / IP
File based SHA-256 bucket tracking
Exceeded -> 429 with retry-after"]
        SG["SetupGuardMiddleware
Blocks /configuration/environment-setup if IS_CONFIGURED
Prevents rerunning Setup wizard"]
    end

    RL --> CTRL(["Controller::method(Request)"])

security_headers.behaviour: Emits CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy and X-Content-Type-Options. Generates the CSP nonce in $_SERVER['CSP_NONCE'].

cors.behaviour: Reads effective CORS config, returns 204 for OPTIONS preflight before route matching and validates Origin against the allowlist using fnmatch().

canonical_path.behaviour: Normalizes non-canonical request paths before route dispatch.

websocket_boot.behaviour: Feature-gated by effective websocket config and auto-starts the websocket process only when enabled.

tenancy_context.behaviour: Resolves tenant context before setup, throttle and CSRF enforcement.

setup.behaviour: Redirects to /configuration/environment-setup when IS_CONFIGURED is false and is a no-op otherwise.

csrf.behaviour: Validates CSRF tokens for mutating verbs using X-CSRF-TOKEN or csrf_token.

auth.behaviour: Requires an authenticated session and preserves the intended URL in ?redirect=.

guest.behaviour: Requires an unauthenticated session and redirects authenticated users to /.

role.behaviour: Calls Gate::allows() with the configured role and returns 403 on denial.

login_throttle.behaviour: Allows 5 login attempts per 10-minute window per IP using file-based SHA-256 buckets.

request_throttle.behaviour: Generic mutating-request limiter keyed by actor, method and path; persists counters on disk and returns 429 outside development.

setup_guard.behaviour: Allows first-run setup access and, after configuration, requires an authenticated privileged actor.

layers.title: Component Layer Map
layers.description: Catalyst uses a strict layering convention. Each layer may depend only on lower layers. Cross-cutting helpers such as Logger, Translator and ConfigManager remain shared services. The diagram stays high level and the current runtime inventory is listed below it.
layers.diagram:
flowchart TD
    ENTRY["Entry Points\npublic/index.php + public/cli.php"]
    BOOT["Bootstrap\nboot-core constants, env, config, routes and cache"]
    CORE["Core Runtime\napp/Kernel.php + app/Framework + app/Helpers"]
    DOC["Canonical HTML Document\nboot-core/template/document.phtml + shell.phtml"]
    UI["Single Frontend Runtime + Global UI Capabilities\npublic/assets/js/catalyst/runtime/ui-runtime.js"]
    FW["Framework Owners\nConfiguration, Users, Account, Workspaces,\nOperations, API, Auth, Notification, DevTools, Demo UI"]
    APP["Derived Application Extensions\nRepository/App"]

    ENTRY --> BOOT --> CORE
    CORE --> DOC --> UI
    CORE --> FW
    CORE --> APP
    FW --> DOC
    APP --> DOC
    APP -. consumes framework contracts .-> FW
layers.loaded_sections_empty: No JSON config sections loaded yet. Setup is still pending.

boot.title: Boot Layer
boot.description: Bootstrap assets that execute before normal request dispatch begins.

core.title: Core Runtime
core.description: Live engine code under app/ executed by the request pipeline.

helpers.title: Cross-Cutting Helpers
helpers.description: Shared services and utilities accessed from multiple runtime layers.

modules.title: Repository Modules
modules.description: Installable framework surfaces currently present under Repository/Framework/.

badges.auth: Auth: Login, Logout, Register, MFA, Password Reset, Social Auth, Email Verification
badges.settings: Configuration: setup, appearance, health, feature flags and plugins
badges.devtools: DevTools: TestFeatures, UML, DB Test/Reset, ORM, Upload, RBAC, I18n, Mail, Infra, Forms, Flash, Modal, Toaster
badges.roles: Users: users, roles, permissions, hierarchy and administrative recovery
badges.common: Canonical framework owners plus app extensions consume one document, shell and UI runtime
